description: Dotfiles changes accumulate and require manual sessions to group, commit, and push. Multiple sessions show the user asking Claude to commit dotfiles or setting up cron agents to do so, but the cycle keeps recurring.
Changes made on live Mint system are not automatically captured in the NixOS config. User has to manually run drift-analysis sessions to find what would be lost on a fresh install.
Autodoro (pomodoro/break tool) repeatedly requires manual intervention: restarting after crashes, adjusting break-screen behavior, adding exclusions for new apps. Each tweak is a separate session.

Security hardening and dependency auditing across Claude plugins and tools is done manually across multiple sessions. User asked for weekly cron to check deps via deps.dev, but the broader pattern of security-scan-then-fix repeats.
User worries about losing impermanent changes on NixOS. Asked for hourly agent to encode ephemeral changes into nix config and commit, but setup itself required manual multi-session effort.
